steps.title: Recognize that both equations represent the same line
steps.content: Check if the second equation is a multiple of the first. Dividing $15x + 24y = 27$ by 3 gives $5x + 8y = 9$, which is identical to the first equation. Since both equations represent the same line, any point on this line satisfies both equations, and there are infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Solve for y in terms of x
Parameterize the line with x = r
steps.content:
For any real number $r$, if $x = r$, then $y = -\frac{5r}{8} + \frac{9}{8}$, giving the point $(r, -\frac{5r}{8} + \frac{9}{8})$.

Verify by substituting into the first equation: $5r + 8\left(-\frac{5r}{8} + \frac{9}{8}\right) = 5r - 5r + 9 = 9$ ✓
That matches choice A — A is correct.
